mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized supply-demand shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × HOUST decline) shows no statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or the combined signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive Fed tightening disproportionately compresses equity valuations by raising the equity risk premium, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 240 and FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month, or the interaction effect disappears when restricting the sample to periods with CPIAUCSL < 230.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income directly boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS growth rates show no positive correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index below 105 signals capacity utilization stress that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the 105 threshold, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and declining real disposable income indicate a weakening demand-labor feedback loop that reduces corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in PAYEMS and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared compared to a single-proxy model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, housing starts act as a leading indicator of credit demand; a sharp drop in housing activity while GDP remains elevated signals a credit crunch that abruptly halts equity rallies, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The negative return prediction fails when GDPC1 exceeds 16200 and HOUST declines, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline GDPC1-only model in walk-forward tests.
